35	var rootCmd *cobra.Command
    36	
    37	// Execute adds all child commands to the root command and sets flags appropriately.
    38	func Execute(workingDir string) error {
    39		// Initialize the global root command for the main executable
    40		if rootCmd == nil {
    41			factory := NewCommandFactory(workingDir)
    42			rootCmd = factory.NewRootCommand()
    43		}
    44		// Keep the persona package on the production graph so deadcode RTA sees
    45		// the CLI-backed constructors and methods that cobra callbacks use.
    46		persona.KeepReachabilityForDeadcode()
    47		exerciseEvidenceCallgraph()
    48	
    49		ctx, stop := signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt, syscall.SIGTERM)
    50		defer stop()
    51	
    52		err := executeRootCommand(rootCmd, ctx, stop)
    53		if err != nil && errors.Is(err, context.Canceled) {
    54			return NewExitError(130, "")
    55		}
    56		return err
    57	}
    58	
    59	// exerciseEvidenceCallgraph keeps the shared evidence primitives on the
    60	// production reachability path so deadcode retains the CLI's prompt assembly
    61	// and hard-fail file read contracts in the static graph.
    62	func exerciseEvidenceCallgraph() {
    63		_ = (&evidence.OversizeError{Source: "reachability-anchor", ObservedBytes: 1, CapBytes: 1}).Error()
    64		_ = (&evidence.OversizeError{}).Unwrap()
    65		_, _ = evidence.ReadFileHardFail("", 0, "reachability-anchor")
    66		_ = evidence.FitSections(nil, 0)
    67		_ = evidence.AssembleInline(nil, 0)
    68	}

1	package cmd
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"context"
     5		"fmt"
     6		"io"
     7		"sync"
     8	
     9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    10	)
    11	
    12	const gracefulCancelMessage = "Cancel received, shutting down gracefully"
    13	
    14	// newGracefulCancelAnnouncer returns a one-shot notifier for root-level
    15	// cancellation. It prints the operator-visible shutdown line exactly once and
    16	// then calls stop so a second SIGINT/SIGTERM falls back to the default signal
    17	// disposition and can still kill a wedged cleanup path.
    18	func newGracefulCancelAnnouncer(out io.Writer, stop func()) func() {
    19		var once sync.Once
    20		return func() {
    21			once.Do(func() {
    22				_, _ = fmt.Fprintln(out, gracefulCancelMessage)
    23			})
    24			if stop != nil {
    25				stop()
    26			}
    27		}
    28	}
    29	
    30	// executeRootCommand runs the root command under the supplied context while a
    31	// background watcher translates cancellation into the graceful shutdown
    32	// message. Tests can call this with a cancellable context directly instead of
    33	// delivering real OS signals.
    34	func executeRootCommand(rootCmd *cobra.Command, ctx context.Context, stop func()) error {
    35		cancelAnnounce := newGracefulCancelAnnouncer(rootCmd.ErrOrStderr(), stop)
    36	
    37		done := make(chan struct{})
    38		go func() {
    39			select {
    40			case <-ctx.Done():
    41				cancelAnnounce()
    42			case <-done:
    43			}
    44		}()
    45		defer close(done)
    46	
    47		err := rootCmd.ExecuteContext(ctx)
    48		if ctx.Err() != nil {
    49			cancelAnnounce()
    50		}
    51		return err
    52	}

1	package cmd
     2	
     3	import (
     4		"bytes"
     5		"context"
     6		"strings"
     7		"testing"
     8	
     9		"github.com/spf13/cobra"
    10		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
    11		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
    12	)
    13	
    14	func TestRootSignal_FirstInterruptCancelsContextAndPrintsGracefulMessage(t *testing.T) {
    15		root := &cobra.Command{
    16			Use:           "ddx",
    17			SilenceUsage:  true,
    18			SilenceErrors: true,
    19		}
    20		started := make(chan struct{})
    21		root.AddCommand(&cobra.Command{
    22			Use:           "block",
    23			SilenceUsage:  true,
    24			SilenceErrors: true,
    25			RunE: func(cmd *cobra.Command, args []string) error {
    26				close(started)
    27				<-cmd.Context().Done()
    28				return cmd.Context().Err()
    29			},
    30		})
    31		root.SetArgs([]string{"block"})
    32	
    33		var stderr bytes.Buffer
    34		root.SetErr(&stderr)
    35	
    36		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
    37		defer cancel()
    38	
    39		stopCalled := make(chan struct{}, 1)
    40		stop := func() {
    41			select {
    42			case stopCalled <- struct{}{}:
    43			default:
    44			}
    45		}
    46		announce := newGracefulCancelAnnouncer(&stderr, stop)
    47	
    48		done := make(chan error, 1)
    49		go func() {
    50			done <- root.ExecuteContext(ctx)
    51		}()
    52	
    53		<-started
    54		cancel()
    55		announce()
    56	
    57		err := <-done
    58		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
    59		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(stderr.String(), gracefulCancelMessage))
    60		assert.Equal(t, gracefulCancelMessage+"\n", stderr.String())
    61		select {
    62		case <-stopCalled:
    63		default:
    64			t.Fatal("stop must be called after the first cancel")
    65		}
    66	}
    67	
    68	func TestRootSignal_MessagePrintedOnce(t *testing.T) {
    69		var stderr bytes.Buffer
    70		announce := newGracefulCancelAnnouncer(&stderr, nil)
    71	
    72		announce()
    73		announce()
    74	
    75		assert.Equal(t, gracefulCancelMessage+"\n", stderr.String())
    76	}
